items.original_body:
Running `company-complete` with the point at the beginning of a line, when in `sh-mode`, triggers a long list of completion options. Is this by design? Is there a way of forcing `company-complete` to ignore the empty string?

I ended up running into trouble when using `company-complete` in `sh-mode` with `tab-always-indent` set to `'complete`. Trying to press `tab` at the beginning of a line leads to a somewhat useless list of completion options. This doesn't happen when in e.g. `org-mode`---I presume this is because when I run `company-complete` on the empty string in `org-mode` this results in a `No completion found`message. 

The following seems to reproduce the behavior I'm talking about. 

- Start vanilla emacs: `emacs -q`
- Manually install `company` and call `company-mode`.
- Enter `sh-mode` in an empty buffer.
- Eval `(setq tab-always-indent 'complete)`.
- Press TAB at the beginning of a line.
items.mutated_body: Running `company-complete` with the point at the beginning of a line, when in `sh-mode`, triggers a long list of completion options. Is this by design? Is there a way of forcing `company-complete` to ignore the empty string? 下

items.original_title: Automatically move spawn point if spawn point isn't valid
items.original_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
If you die and haven't changed your spawnpoint using a bed you always respawn at the same position. This becomes bad if you destroy your spawn island to move it lower in the world causing you to always drop down with the potential to be stuck in a death loop.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be nice to have a system in place which checks if the normal spawn point is secure and if not it spawns you at the next best location.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Maybe grant a resistance effect after death but that wouldn't solve the issue if there are no blocks below to fall on.
items.mutated_title: Automatically move spawn point if spawn point isn't valid
items.mutated_body: If you die and haven't changed the spawnpoint using a bed, you always respawn at the same position. This becomes bad if you destroy the spawn island to move it lower in the world, causing you to always drop down with the potential to be stuck in a death loop. Describe the solution you'd like. It would be nice to have a system in place which checks if the normal spawn point is secure and if not it spawns you at the next best location. Describe alternatives you've considered. Maybe grant a resistance effect after death but that wouldn't solve the issue if there are no blocks below to fall on.

items.original_title: New applications are not getting listed
items.original_body:
Running Cockpit using 238.1 tag
OS: Ubuntu 21.04
Page: Applications

Steps:
1. Installed Apache NiFi on my system.
2. In source code, created a directory named nifi under pkg.
3. Added two files under this directory. 
One is the normal **index.html** and another with the name **org.cockpit-project.cockpit-nifi.metainfo.xml** with the content as shown in attached image.

 **org.cockpit-project.cockpit-nifi.metainfo.xml**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39826327/117310069-9bfd4800-aea0-11eb-8f9d-e0a121eff86a.png)

4. Added link to above xml file in Makefile.am
**Makefile.am**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39826327/117309912-81c36a00-aea0-11eb-9a11-35ebcb67c72a.png)
items.mutated_title: New applications are not getting listed

items.original_title: Update libs\class-oik-update.php to test oik issue 11
items.original_body: In bobbingwide/oik/issues/11 I've updated the shared library file `libs\class-oik-update.php` to remove from the list of plugins to update any plugin that's a symlinked plugin or a Git repo. \r\n\r\nBecause I was using oik and oik-bwtrace I updated the file in oik-bwtrace. I need to test in a safe environment so need to commit the changes to oik-bwtrace, create a new version and install it separately - as a clone of the master git repo. Other plugins can be symlinked to versions where it doesn't matter if they get destroyed.
items.mutated_title: https://discord.gg/qCJ95FVbzRčĊ Update libs\class-oik-update.php to test oik issue 11

I need to test in a safe environment so need to commit the changes to oik-bwtrace, create a new version and install it separately - as a clone of the master git repo. Other plugins can be symlinked to versions where it doesn't matter if they get destroyed.
